facts.text: Denver was founded in 1858 at the confluence of Cherry Creek and the South Platte River during the Gold Rush.
The city earned the nickname "Mile High City" because its official elevation is exactly one mile (5,280 feet) above sea level.
Denver hosted the 1976 Winter Olympics bid, withdrew in 1972 after voters rejected public funding, making it the only city to back out after winning a bid.
Union Station, rebuilt and refurbished, became a transportation hub and now hosts restaurants, bars, and a hotel, reflecting Denver's rail heritage.
The University of Denver, founded in 1864, was the first higher‑education institution in the city.
Qwest Corporation, founded in Denver in 1911 as Mountain States Telephone & Telegraph Company, is now part of Lumen Technologies.
